summary: A doctest in `doc/callbacks.rst` failed because the expected output shows a progress bar (`LogisticRegression - fit ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 100% 0:03:12`) but the actual output is just `LogisticRegression()` (the repr of the fitted estimator). The doctest for `estimator.fit(X, y)` was not marked with `# doctest: +SKIP` even though the preceding `set_callbacks` call was skipped, so the callback was never registered and no progress bar was displayed.
root_cause: The doctest in `doc/callbacks.rst` at line 48 expects a progress bar output (`LogisticRegression - fit ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 100% 0:03:12`) from `estimator.fit(X, y)`, but the actual output is `LogisticRegression()`. The `set_callbacks` call on line 39 is marked `# doctest: +SKIP` so the progress bar callback is never registered, causing the subsequent `fit` call to produce no progress bar output — only the default repr.

evidence.reason: Identifies the failing doctest as being in callbacks.rst
The set_callbacks call is skipped, so the progress bar is never registered on the estimator
The doctest expected a progress bar output but got only the estimator repr, confirming the callback was not active
Pinpoints the exact file and line number of the doctest failure
Confirms the test identifier and that it is the sole failing test
Shows this is the only failure in the doc test run

suggested_fix: Add `# doctest: +SKIP` to the `estimator.fit(X, y)` line in `doc/callbacks.rst` (line 48) so it is also skipped when the `set_callbacks` call is skipped, or update the doctest to not depend on the progress bar output (e.g., use `# doctest: +ELLIPSIS` with an appropriate expected pattern, or restructure so both the `set_callbacks` and `fit` calls are either both skipped or both executed).
